items.hook: NEC Corporation is organized as the first Japanese joint venture with foreign capital.
items.body: NEC Corporation is a Japanese multinational information technology corporation headquartered at the NEC Supertower in Minato, Tokyo, Japan. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Aangan, alternatively spelled Angan, is a period novel by Pakistani novelist and short story writer Khadija Mastoor.
items.body: Published in 1962, it is hailed as a masterpiece of Urdu literature. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: On March 1, 2018, it was announced that she would become the first transgender person to perform a principal role on an American operatic stage.
items.body: Lucia Lucas is an American transgender baritone. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The Red Mouth by Sheila Armstrong review – profound exploration of Ireland’s deep time
items.body:
Sinister bogland discoveries haunt the intersecting lives of four characters in this meditative, exquisitely written tale

Almost 14% of Ireland is bog: vast swathes of moss-carpeted land, below which layers of ancient history have

items.hook: The 7.0 Mw Papua New Guinea earthquake triggers a tsunami that destroys ten villages in Papua New Guinea, killing up to 2,700 people, and leaving several thousand injured.
items.body: The 1998 Papua New Guinea earthquake occurred on July 17 with a moment magnitude of 7.0 and a maximum Mercalli intensity of VIII (Severe). One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: What we’re reading: writers and readers on the books they enjoyed in June
items.body:
Candice Carty-Williams, Patrick Freyne and Guardian readers discuss the titles they have read over the last month. Join the conversation in the comments

I just finished reading Wimmy Road Boyz by Sufiyaan Salam. I

items.hook: Port Chicago disaster: Near the San Francisco Bay, two ships laden with ammunition for the war explode in Port Chicago, California, killing 320.
items.body: The Port Chicago disaster was a deadly munitions explosion of the ship SS E. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The largest number of United States midshipman casualties in a single event results from an aircraft crash in Florida, killing 44.
items.body: A midshipman is an officer of the lowest rank in the Royal Navy, United States Navy, and many Commonwealth navies. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Paralympian Dame Sarah Storey joins Strictly line-up
items.body: Dame Sarah, who has won 30 medals for Great Britain, is the seventh celebrity to be confirmed for Strictly Come Dancing.

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: The Fog is a horror novel by English writer James Herbert, published in 1975.
items.body: It is about a deadly fog that drives its victims insane when they come into contact with it. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Battle of Castillon: The last battle of the Hundred Years' War, the French under Jean Bureau defeat the English under the Earl of Shrewsbury, who is killed in the battle in Gascony.
items.body: The Battle of Castillon was a battle between the forces of England and France which took place on 17 July 1453 in Gascony near the town of Castillon-sur-Dordogne. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The Amber House Trilogy is a series of young adult books by American author Kelly Moore and her daughters Tucker Reed and Larkin Reed.
items.body: The first book in the series, Amber House, was published on October 1, 2012, through Arthur A. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Douglas Corrigan takes off from Brooklyn to fly the "wrong way" to Ireland and becomes known as "Wrong Way" Corrigan.
items.body: Douglas Corrigan was an American aviator, nicknamed "Wrong Way" in 1938. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Kit Connor and Joe Locke: 'Making a film like Heartstopper will forever be important'
items.body: The Netflix movie brings Alice Oseman's popular screen adaptation to a conclusion.

items.hook: The Goose Girl is a 1915 American silent drama film directed by Frederick A.
items.body: Thomson and distributed by Paramount Pictures. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Lauren Laverne diagnosed with blood and bone marrow disorder
items.body: The broadcaster reveals she has smouldering myeloma, which can develop into a type of blood cancer.

items.hook: Seth Meyers on Trump 2.0: ‘Every day is a constant stream of humiliation’
items.body:
Late-night host criticised the Trump administration’s obsessions with testosterone and wet magnets as more important issues are ignored

Seth Meyers took aim at Donald Trump’s bizarre priorities and obsessions as many Americans cont

items.hook: Maigret's Memoirs is a novel by the Belgian writer Georges Simenon.
items.body: Unlike other Maigret novels, there is no plot; Jules Maigret himself writes about his life and work, and about his relation with the novelist Georges Simenon. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Brenda Fricker, Oscar winner for My Left Foot, dies aged 81
items.body:
The acclaimed Irish actor started her career in Coronation Street and Casualty before a string of high-profile Hollywood roles
